case_title: LILY TIEN VS. YU YUN TIEN ET AL

docket_entries.description: NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF
NOTICE OF PENDENCY OF ACTION (TRANSACTION ID # 60309634) FILED BY CROSS COMPLAINANT TIEN, YU YUN

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, CROSS DEFENDANT LILY TIEN'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS BROUGHT BY A NON-PARTY AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND A NON-PARTY. CROSS-COMPLAINANT DOES NOT CITE ANY AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT "AN INDIVIDUAL" AND "A TRUSTEE" ARE THE SAME PARTY BY VIRTUE OF BEING "REAL PARTIES IN INTEREST" EACH IN THEIR OWN RIGHT AS TO SEPARATE CLAIMS. CROSS-COMPLAINT DOES NOT ARISE OUT OF THE SAME TRANSACTION/OCCURRENCE AS THE COMPLAINT. CROSS-COMPLAINANT DOES NOT SUBSTANTIVELY ADDRESS CROSS-DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENT THAT CROSS-CLAIMS BELONG IN PROBATE DEPARTMENT. MEET AND CONFER REQUIREMENTS HAVE BEEN SUFFICIENTLY COMPLIED WITH. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC3.1312(B). JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED (D501)

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-13-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT LILY TIEN's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is Sustained without leave to amend. The cross-complaint is brought by a non-party against Plaintiff and a non-party. Cross-complainant does not cite any authority for the proposition that "an individual" and "a trustee" are the same party by virtue of being "real parties in interest" each in their own right as to separate claims. Cross-complaint does not arise out of the same transaction/occurrence as the Complaint. Cross-complainant does not substantively address cross-defendant's argument that cross-claims belong in Probate Department. Meet and confer requirements have been sufficiently complied with. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported (D501)
